summary: Are you a woman who feels hesitant about managing money? Wondering how to start your financial journey with confidence? In this episode of This or That Podcast, Bhartendra Singh sits down with Lisa Pallavi Barbora, Certified Financial Coach and author of Money and Her, to talk about why women must take charge of their finances.

chapters.label: Why managing money feels hard for women – Lisa’s story and why she wrote Money and Her
Why many women hesitate to take charge of their money
The 3 big reasons: Fear of numbers, guilt, and family responsibilities
The self-doubt that stops women from learning about money
How Money and Her is structured: Real stories of career breaks, fear, and making a comeback
How money affects relationships and why couples should talk openly
Build awareness before you talk about money with your partner
The 5 simple numbers every woman (and man) should know
From saving to investing: How to shift your mindset
The 50-30-20 rule for managing your money
Why everyone needs an emergency fund
Why health insurance and term life insurance are must-haves
Understanding asset classes: Equity, real estate, and mutual funds
Quick summary: Save, invest smartly, have a plan, and choose the right tools
Should women have separate wills? Should couples plan for retirement together or separately?
Real stories from Money and Her: Couples, joint finances, and transparency
Final message: Women must take charge, change their mindset, and learn

tool.label: The Fork Test
tool.reason: Meet your money archetype in nine taps, then see which habits to lean into and which to drop.

disclaimer: This video is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial, legal, or investment advice. Please consult a qualified financial advisor before making any financial decisions. These are personal opinions and do not represent any company or organization.

seo.title: Women, Money & Mindset: Lisa Pallavi Barbora on Taking Charge of Your Finances | EP 09
seo.description: Are you a woman who feels hesitant about managing money? Wondering how to start your financial journey with confidence? In this episode of This or That

takeaways.text: Lisa Pallavi Barbora traces women's hesitation with money to three forces: fear of numbers, guilt, and the weight of family responsibilities, often compounded by self-doubt about whether they can even learn it.
Awareness comes before conversation: build your own understanding of your numbers first, and money talks with a partner get easier and more honest.
The episode names five simple numbers every woman and man should know, then pairs them with the 50-30-20 rule as a plain starting frame for managing income.
An emergency fund, health insurance, and term life insurance come first: they are the foundations to secure before shifting mindset from saving to investing.
Money and Her collects 15 real stories of women navigating career breaks, fear, and comebacks, landing on one message: taking charge starts with a mindset shift and a willingness to learn.
